29.reason: The recursive call passes root->lch by value, but the returned pointer is not assigned back to root->lch, so the new node is never linked to the parent.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  The recursive call passes root->lch by value, but the returned pointer is not assigned back to root->lch, so the new node is never linked to the parent.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  Error: The return value of add is not assigned to root->lch, so the new node is not linked to the parent. Should be: root->lch = add(root->lch, x);
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Should assign result to root->lch and return root. E.g., root->lch = add(root->lch, x); return root;
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  The recursive call does not assign the returned subtree to root->lch, so the new node is not linked. This causes root to be overwritten by the last inserted node.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is not assigned back to root->lch, so the newly created node is lost and the tree structure is not updated.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.5 , reason:  The return value of the recursive call is not assigned back to root->lch, so the new node is lost and the tree structure is not updated.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [The recursive result is not assigned to root->lch, so the tree structure is not properly built.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [The recursive result is not assigned back to root->lch, so the left subtree is never updated.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Should assign result to root->rch and return root. E.g., root->rch = add(root->rch, x); return root;
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Same issue: the result of the recursive call is not assigned to root->rch, breaking the tree structure.
